reports.title: OpenAI Wants Immunity. Anthropic Wants Accountability. One AI Bill Will Decide Which Vision Wins.
reports.subtitle: Illinois SB 3444 has cracked the AI industry's safety consensus in two, and the fault line runs straight through the question of who pays when an AI kills someone.

reports.title: The AI That Can Break Into Banks Exists. The Rules to Stop It Don't.
reports.subtitle: Anthropic's Mythos model completed a full enterprise network breach in simulation and scored 73% on expert cyberattack tasks. No one outside Anthropic can access it. No regulator has authority over it.

reports.title: SCOTUS Is About to Rule on Birthright Citizenship. The Market Says 94% Chance They Strike It Down.
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court heard final arguments on Trump's executive order redefining citizenship. The constitutional question is live. The outcome almost certainly isn't.

reports.title: Europe Is Building a NATO Without the US. Germany Just Agreed to Help.
reports.subtitle: European allies have moved from theorizing about American withdrawal to actively designing command structures that exclude Washington. The 80-year alliance is splitting in plain sight.

reports.title: The Iran War Is Costing the World Enough to Buy a Recession. Nobody Is Presenting the Bill.
reports.subtitle: The IMF says the war has already erased gains from the AI boom and is pushing the world toward its adverse scenario. The markets price a 28.5% chance of US recession. The price is rising.

reports.title: Ohio Voters Put Abortion Rights in the Constitution. The Legislature Is Systematically Dismantling Them Anyway.
reports.subtitle: The SHE WINS Act is the 11th Ohio bill targeting abortion since voters enshrined reproductive freedom in the state constitution by 57% in 2023. The real test is whether the Ohio Supreme Court will honor the referendum.

reports.title: Trump Threatens to Fire Powell. The Market Says He Won't Get What He Wants Either Way.
reports.subtitle: The Federal Reserve chair's term ends May 15, but his replacement can't get confirmed, a criminal investigation is blocking the vote, and Powell says he's staying. No one is getting a rate cut.

reports.title: The Hormuz Blockade Is Not About Iran. It's About What China Will Agree to Before Trump Arrives in Beijing.
reports.subtitle: The US naval embargo that's halting oil tankers in the world's most critical shipping lane has a secondary audience of one: Xi Jinping, who needs that oil and who Trump is visiting in six weeks.

reports.title: The EU Just Built the Infrastructure to Ban Children from Social Media. The Harder Question Is Who Decides What a Child Is.
reports.subtitle: Von der Leyen announced an EU age verification app today. Canada is going further and applying the same logic to AI chatbots. The technology exists. The politics are just beginning.

reports.title: Orban Lost His Election. His Israel Policy Did Not.
reports.subtitle: Hungary's incoming leader Peter Magyar says he will keep the veto on EU sanctions against Israel. Five countries want to punish Israel for Gaza. One country can stop all of them. And the EU is starting to ask whether the unanimity rule should survive this.

reports.title: A Judge Froze RFK Jr.'s Vaccine Changes. He's Already Working Around the Ruling.
reports.subtitle: US District Judge Brian Murphy blocked all Trump-era changes to the national vaccine schedule. The same day, HHS announced new criteria for the advisory panel RFK Jr. had already stacked. The courts and the executive are playing whack-a-mole with public health infrastructure.
